title: The One With The Worst Best Man Ever

jokes.text: That was the 10th time I've peed since I've been here. It's also, like, the 10th time you've told us. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Pregnant Phoebe announces her bathroom frequency
jokes.punchline: Rachel points out she's also announced it the same number of times
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good economy. The meta-humor of calling out repetitive announcements works well.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I have to pee again. That's the third time this hour!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you've announced that you have to pee three times this hour.

jokes.text: Yeah, oh, I'm sorry, it must be hard to hear. I'll tell you, it's easier having babies playing 'Bring in 'da Noise, Bring in 'da Funk' on your bladder.
jokes.setup: Discussion about pregnancy discomfort
jokes.punchline: Comparing fetal movement to the Broadway show title about noise and funk
jokes.explanation: Clever reference that perfectly captures the physical sensation. Very Phoebe-specific humor with strong visual imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: The baby's been kicking so much, it feels like there's a whole Broadway show going on in there.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That sounds uncomfortable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: It is! It's like 'Stomp'.

jokes.text: I am so sick of being pregnant. The only happiness I get is from a cup of coffee which of course is decaf because, oh, I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Phoebe complains about pregnancy restrictions
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of being denied her one joy because of the condition causing her misery
jokes.explanation: Good observation about pregnancy catch-22s, delivered with Phoebe's characteristic frustrated sarcasm.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't have coffee because of the baby, I can't have sushi because of the baby, I can't have a nice soft cheese because of the baby. The only thing that makes me happy is eating, and I can't do that because of the baby!

jokes.text: So, uh, Pheebs, honey, how are those mood swings coming? I haven't really had any yet.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks about mood swings after Phoebe's extended complaining
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claims she hasn't had mood swings despite just having a major one
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing and structure. The immediate contradiction is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Have you been having mood swings?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No! I haven't had mood swings!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Phoebe, you just had one.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No I didn't!

jokes.text: Yes, yes. A thousand times, yes.
jokes.setup: Ross shows the wedding ring
jokes.punchline: Joey dramatically accepts as if being proposed to
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey misunderstanding with physical comedy. Character-appropriate but predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Yes! Yes, I will marry you!

jokes.text: Oh, it's awkward. It's awkward, it's awkward.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions picking a best man
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately recognizes and announces the social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Very Chandler-like meta-commentary. The repetition adds comedic weight and quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I need to pick a best man.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh, wow. Okay, so you're not asking me?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I haven't decided yet.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could this BE any more awkward?

jokes.text: You can be the best man when I get married. I'm never gonna get to be a best man.
jokes.setup: Chandler offers Joey a future best man role
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately realizes the flaw in this plan
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical turnaround that reveals Joey's self-awareness about his romantic prospects. Well-structured.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, I was thinking... when I get married, I want you to be my best man.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, that's great! Wait... you're gonna get married?

jokes.text: I'm not even getting married! Okay, this is a question for science-fiction writers.
jokes.setup: Complex best man arrangements being discussed
jokes.punchline: Chandler declares the scenario too impossible for reality
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke that perfectly captures the absurdity. The sci-fi reference is unexpected and clever.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This is impossible. This is like asking me to choose between Monica and Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Actually, no—I've done that. This is harder.

jokes.text: Yes. Shame about you, though.
jokes.setup: Ross picks Joey as best man
jokes.punchline: Joey's mock sympathy for Chandler
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery. Joey's faux-sympathy while celebrating his win is character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, I'm honored. But I gotta say, I feel really bad for Chandler.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because when this wedding's over, you're gonna be married. And I'm still gonna be Joey Tribbiani.

jokes.text: It's not kicking me. It's kicking one of the other babies. Oh. Don't make me come in there!
jokes.setup: Discussion about baby kicking
jokes.punchline: Phoebe threatens to intervene in fetal sibling rivalry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Treating fetuses like misbehaving children is perfectly Phoebe and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, the baby's kicking! Feel it!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, but I think the other one's jealous. If they don't stop fighting in there, I'm gonna reach in and separate them!

jokes.text: Hormones. What Phoebe meant was how come you're having a party and we're not invited?
jokes.setup: Joey tries to leave quickly after mentioning a party
jokes.punchline: Monica 'translates' Phoebe's aggressive response into a reasonable question
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's diplomatic skills while acknowledging Phoebe's pregnancy mood.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, what did she say?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I said, if you leave this apartment, I will rip your arms off and beat you with them!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She's asking if you'll bring back some beer.

jokes.text: Are you bachelors? No. Are you strippers? No. Then you're not invited.
jokes.setup: Women want to know why they're not invited to bachelor party
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation of bachelor party requirements
jokes.explanation: Clean logical structure with good economy. Joey's literal thinking creates the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why aren't we invited to the bachelor party?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: It's a bachelor party. You have to be a bachelor.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: We can be bachelors.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, you have to be single guys. That's what a bachelor is.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So if we pretend to be single guys—

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You can't pretend. You either are or you aren't. I don't make the rules.

jokes.text: Wow. That so doesn't sound like something I want to do. Later.
jokes.setup: Monica mentions baby shower for Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate and honest dismissal
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey character moment. The blunt honesty about not wanting to attend a baby shower is authentic.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A baby shower? I'm not going to a baby shower. I don't even go to my own mother's birthday parties.

jokes.text: I can't believe I'm going to have a party! Ha, ha. That's so great. A party! Yay! Ha, ha. [CRYING]
jokes.setup: Phoebe excitedly celebrates having a party
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks into tears for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Perfect mood swing representation. The immediate emotional whiplash is both uncomfortable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm so excited! I'm having a party!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I just... I can't... *breaks into tears* Why am I crying?!

jokes.text: And I'm invite Gunther because, well, we've been talking pretty loud. I'll be there.
jokes.setup: Joey planning bachelor party guest list
jokes.punchline: Gunther's matter-of-fact acceptance based on overhearing
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about party planning logistics. Gunther's deadpan response works well.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So I'm thinking about who to invite to my bachelor party, and I want to make sure I get all the right guys.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: I'll be there.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, what? I didn't even ask you.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: I heard you talking about it. I'm in.

jokes.text: Just the dinosaur dudes.
jokes.setup: Limiting anthropologists but allowing paleontologists
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual reference to paleontologists as 'dinosaur dudes'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Joey terminology. The casual way he refers to Ross's colleagues is endearing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Paleontologists? Yeah, those dinosaur dudes are cool.

jokes.text: Okay, we'll need a six-pack of Zima.
jokes.setup: Planning bachelor party supplies
jokes.punchline: Joey's hilariously inadequate alcohol planning
jokes.explanation: The specificity of Zima and the modest quantity suggests Joey's inexperience with party planning.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, so for the bachelor party, I'm thinking we get some beer, maybe some wine...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, that's it? That's your big plan?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? I went to the store, I got a six-pack!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: A six-pack? Joey, there's gonna be like thirty guys there!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So? We can always get more... I mean, I can always drink more.

jokes.text: I don't think it's gonna be that difficult, considering this one won't take place in the basement of a Pizza Hut. Oh, I'm Ross, I'm Ross. I'm too good for the Hut. I'm too good for the Hut.
jokes.setup: Ross dismisses Joey's previous bachelor party location
jokes.punchline: Joey's mocking impression of Ross being pretentious
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Joey's defensive impression. The Pizza Hut detail is both pathetic and endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, so now you're too good for Atlantic City? What, you want to go somewhere fancy like the Hamptons or something?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: "Oh, I'm Ross Geller, I have a PhD in paleontology, I only celebrate life's milestones in places with Michelin stars."

jokes.text: There's gonna be strippers there. He didn't say anything about no strippers.
jokes.setup: Ross explicitly said no strippers
jokes.punchline: Joey's willful misinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey selective hearing. Character-appropriate but somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! I didn't hire a stripper. This is my friend Candy. She's a dancer.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What? You said no strippers. She's a dancer!

jokes.text: He just said, 'No strippers.' Oh. I chose not to hear that.
jokes.setup: Chandler corrects Joey about Ross's stripper rule
jokes.punchline: Joey admits to deliberate selective hearing
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment. The honesty about choosing not to hear inconvenient information is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not a stripper rule, it's a 'we were on a break' rule.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, right, right. I knew that. I just wasn't listening.

jokes.text: Little Village People.
jokes.setup: Looking at tiny outfits for babies
jokes.punchline: Reference to the disco group while describing baby clothes
jokes.explanation: Clever visual pun connecting baby outfits to the iconic disco group. Economical and cute.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, look at these little outfits! They're so tiny!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know! It's like clothes for the Bee Gees!

jokes.text: Except, uh, Phoebe's not gonna be the one who gets to dress them. I guess she's not gonna get to keep the babies.
jokes.setup: Discussion about dressing babies in cute outfits
jokes.punchline: Reality check about surrogacy arrangement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that punctures the baby shower excitement with harsh reality. More uncomfortable than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I know, but she's carrying our baby. The least we can do is make sure she's comfortable and well-dressed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica, she's a surrogate. She's not a life-size doll.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just want everything to be perfect for when the baby arrives.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The baby arrives from inside of her, not from a shopping bag from Baby Gap.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, we are throwing the most depressing baby shower.
jokes.setup: Realization about the surrogacy situation
jokes.punchline: Direct acknowledgment of the party's inherent sadness
jokes.explanation: Good meta-observation about the situation. The direct acknowledgment of the absurdity works well.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so we're throwing a party for someone who's giving away their baby?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: When you say it like that, it sounds depressing.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm not trying to be depressing, I'm just saying — we're celebrating something really sad.

jokes.text: Oh, and somebody can get her those leather pants she's always wanted.
jokes.setup: Planning gifts Phoebe can use post-pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Reference to Phoebe's character trait
jokes.explanation: Character-specific callback that feels organic to the conversation but not particularly funny on its own.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, so I'm thinking we get you some really nice maternity clothes, and then some regular clothes for after the baby.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, that's so sweet! But, um, I'm gonna need clothes that can go from maternity to regular really quickly.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why? How fast do you think you'll lose the weight?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, because I'm gonna forget I'm pregnant and go back to my old lifestyle.

jokes.text: You actually thought it'd make me feel better? To give me something I can't even use for another two months? This sucks! Oh!
jokes.setup: Friends give Phoebe post-pregnancy gifts
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's completely reasonable but devastating critique
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment where Phoebe's logical response makes the friends' plan seem thoughtless.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh wow, thank you so much! A diaper genie! You know, I've always wondered what I'd do if I had a regular genie and I wished for it to become a diaper genie.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And look, a book on how to be a better mother. Wow. Because apparently I'm not doing a good job.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: And this... what is this? A breast pump? Oh great, so now I can be a cow in my spare time.

jokes.text: What's my last name? Central Perk?
jokes.setup: Ross asks Gunther his last name after naming him best man
jokes.punchline: Gunther's confused guess based on his workplace
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing how little Ross knows about Gunther. The workplace-as-surname guess is perfectly naive.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What's your last name?

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Central?

jokes.text: Thanks for not marrying Rachel.
jokes.setup: Gunther expressing gratitude to Ross
jokes.punchline: Reveals his romantic interest in Rachel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther moment. The unexpected honesty about his romantic hopes is both sweet and awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: Thanks, Ross. You know, I've always appreciated how you treat people around here.

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: But I gotta be honest with you... I've been in love with Rachel for years.

jokes.text: And, uh, you know, so is the position of the bride.
jokes.setup: Ross announces best man position is open
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions he also needs a bride
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating humor about Ross's relationship status. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: The best man position is open.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I also need a bride.

jokes.text: Smooth, man. Yeah. You got some chili on your neck.
jokes.setup: Ross makes awkward comment about needing a bride
jokes.punchline: Joey's mock compliment followed by practical observation
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ross's awkwardness with practical Joey observation. The chili detail adds visual comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, that's a great attitude, man. You know, most guys, they see the glass half empty. But you... you see the glass, and you just think about how nice it's gonna look in your wedding china.

jokes.text: Watch this. Stare at the wall. Hardly move. Be white.
jokes.setup: Joey claims his duck is trained
jokes.punchline: Commands that are just the duck's natural behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Joey's pride in 'training' the duck to do what it naturally does is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My duck is fully trained. Watch this. Okay, Waddles, quack!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: See? She quacked on command. Now watch - flap your wings!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: And look at that - she's waddling. Perfectly trained.

jokes.text: You are really good at that.
jokes.setup: Duck performs Joey's 'tricks'
jokes.punchline: Stripper plays along with Joey's delusion
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that validates Joey's absurd claims. Works as setup for the seduction.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: See? He can do all my tricks!

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: Yeah, that duck's got some moves.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, right? He's a natural!

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: So... does he do private parties?

jokes.text: Yeah. I'll let you play with my duck.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to convince stripper to stay
jokes.punchline: Innocent offer with unintentional sexual undertones
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey innuendo that's actually innocent. The double meaning works because it's unintentional.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. Don't go. I can offer you something better.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A real acting job. I know people in the business. We could work together, develop your talent, really bring out what's inside you.

jokes.text: Stripper.
jokes.setup: Chandler wakes up and sees the stripper
jokes.punchline: Simple, matter-of-fact identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan. The single word delivery captures his blasé attitude perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Oh my God, there's a stripper in my apartment!

jokes.text: You lost the ring. You're the worst best man ever.
jokes.setup: Discovery that wedding ring is missing
jokes.punchline: Chandler's harsh but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Straightforward insult that's both mean and true. The timing after Joey's panic works well.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I've lost a lot of things in my life — my hair, my dignity, my will to live — but never a wedding ring.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Although, given my track record, I'm surprised I made it this far without losing Monica.

jokes.text: You slept with the stripper? Of course!
jokes.setup: Chandler asks obvious question about what happened
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Joey's 'of course' reveals his complete lack of awareness about appropriate behavior. Character-perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What do you think happened? She left me.

jokes.text: Well, how is this like that? Well, let's see. It's not really like that. Because, you see, that was an actual problem.
jokes.setup: Phoebe compares Rachel's wedding anxiety to homelessness
jokes.punchline: Phoebe realizes her comparison is completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Phoebe realizes mid-explanation how inappropriate her comparison is. The backtracking is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know, getting married is kind of like being homeless.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? How is that even—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, you have no idea where you're going to end up, and everything you own is in bags... Oh my God, I've been homeless!

jokes.text: And, uh, yours is just, like, a bunch of high-school crap that nobody really gives... Yeah.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to minimize Rachel's problems
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's being mean and stops mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the awkward comparison. Phoebe's self-awareness kicks in just too late.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I mean, come on, Rachel. You're upset about your apartment? At least you have an apartment. I once lived in a box for three months.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A box?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, and before that I lived in a—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm being really mean. I'm sorry. Your problems are valid.

jokes.text: If this isn't an emergency, then what is?
jokes.setup: Joey called 911 about the missing ring
jokes.punchline: Joey's genuine confusion about emergency priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic. His genuine belief that this constitutes an emergency is both absurd and endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, hi, I need to report a missing person.

dialogue.speaker: 911 Operator
dialogue.line: Sir, is this person in immediate danger?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, no, but my girlfriend's engagement ring is missing and that's like, way more important.

dialogue.speaker: 911 Operator
dialogue.line: Sir, 911 is for emergencies.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know! That's why I called!

jokes.text: Yeah, and another one had never been to a party before, so...
jokes.setup: Ross describing his museum colleagues at the bachelor party
jokes.punchline: One colleague's complete social inexperience
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of Ross's nerdy colleagues. The escalation from 'never been to bachelor party' to 'never been to party' works.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So this one colleague, he's never even been to a bachelor party before. I mean, the guy doesn't know how to talk to people. He showed up wearing a fanny pack and immediately asked if there was a gift registry.

jokes.text: That ring, when my grandmother first came to this country, that ring and the clothes on her back were all she had with her.
jokes.setup: Ross describes the ring's sentimental value
jokes.punchline: Sets up the magnitude of Joey's failure
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the escalation. The immigrant story adds weight to make Joey's loss more significant.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: This ring belonged to my grandmother. It's been in my family for generations. It's incredibly valuable, both monetarily and sentimentally.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, I lost it.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You what?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I lost the ring, man. I'm sorry.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey! This is my grandmother's ring! How could you lose it?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't know! One minute I had it, the next minute—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: This is unbelievable. Do you understand what you've done?

jokes.text: So you might say it's a magic ring.
jokes.setup: Ross builds up the ring's family history
jokes.punchline: Caps the buildup with mystical properties
jokes.explanation: Good escalation capping the ring's importance. The 'magic ring' designation maximizes Joey's guilt.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: This ring has been in my family for generations. My great-grandfather gave it to my great-grandmother, and it's been passed down through the family ever since. It's a symbol of love and commitment that has stood the test of time.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: And according to family legend, it has mystical properties. It's said to make whoever wears it irresistible to their soulmate.

jokes.text: Yeah, the stripper stole it.
jokes.setup: Ross builds up ring's incredible significance
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact confession deflates everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation after Ross's buildup. Joey's casual delivery of devastating news is expertly timed and very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I lost it.

jokes.text: Well, I think it all started when you said, 'Hey, Joey, why don't you be my best man.'
jokes.setup: Ross asks how this could happen
jokes.punchline: Joey traces the problem back to Ross's original decision
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shifts blame back to Ross. Joey's logic is flawed but understandable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: How could this happen?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, it all started when you decided to...

jokes.text: Dude, I screwed up. You don't have to turn me in. Not on you, on the stripper.
jokes.setup: Ross threatens to call police
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks he's being arrested, Chandler clarifies
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Joey's guilt complex. The clarification that they're calling about the stripper works.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm calling the police!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I'm being arrested?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, no. He's not arresting you. He's just calling them.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, thank God. For a second there I thought I was going to jail.

jokes.text: I said, 'You talk to the police, you tell them I'm missing a ring.'
jokes.setup: Company threatens to call police on Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's response treats it like the police work for him
jokes.explanation: Great Joey logic where he treats a threat as an opportunity. His confidence in police priorities is endearing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Call the police? Go ahead! I know the commissioner!

jokes.text: Oh, that sounds like fun, but we got a ring to find!
jokes.setup: Ross suggests calling stripper to his office
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic prioritization
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm about Ross's misplaced priorities. Good reality check moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, because that's what this office needs. A stripper. Not better lighting, not a working coffee machine, not a stapler that actually staples. A stripper.

jokes.text: Well, you can't have the baby here! I haven't sterilized the apartment since the guys moved out!
jokes.setup: Phoebe might go into labor
jokes.punchline: Monica's cleaning-obsessed logic about sterilization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment. Her germaphobia takes priority over the emergency, which is very her.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: If Phoebe goes into labor, we need to make sure everything is sterile. I've already sanitized the apartment three times today.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? If she goes into labor here, this place will be the cleanest delivery room in all of New York.

jokes.text: You know what? It's okay. I'm gonna boil some water and just rip up some sheets.
jokes.setup: Emergency childbirth situation
jokes.punchline: Rachel's knowledge comes entirely from old movies
jokes.explanation: Good observation about movie clichés for home births. Rachel's confidence in useless knowledge is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I've seen this like a million times. First, we need to boil water.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Why? Why do we need to boil water?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know! But they always do it in the movies!

jokes.text: The Bible? I don't know!
jokes.setup: Monica asks for 'the book' about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Rachel grabs the wrong book in panic
jokes.explanation: Standard panic comedy where someone grabs the wrong item. The Bible choice adds slight absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, do you have the pregnancy book I lent you?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh! Yes, of course!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Here it is!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, this is a cookbook.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, you said you wanted to know what to expect!

jokes.text: You be Mr. Gonzales, and I'll be Mr. Wong. Diverse.
jokes.setup: Chandler assigns fake names for the stripper sting
jokes.punchline: Self-aware comment about their fake diversity
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about tokenistic diversity. Chandler's self-awareness about their obvious fake names works.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, we need to go undercover with fake names. I'll be Sal Fasano.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'll be Adrian Zmed.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'll be Jacques Strappe.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We look like a 1970s diversity poster.

jokes.text: Which one of you guys is Gunther Central Perk?
jokes.setup: Stripper arrives looking for client
jokes.punchline: Uses Gunther's 'last name' from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the earlier Gunther moment. Shows consequences of the fake name confusion.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm looking for a Mr. Bing?

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: That's not... that's Chandler's last name.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: Oh, well do you know where I can find him?

dialogue.speaker: Gunther
dialogue.line: He went to the bathroom.

jokes.text: Way to be cool, man.
jokes.setup: Ross immediately accuses stripper of theft
jokes.punchline: Joey's sarcastic assessment of Ross's interrogation skills
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Ross's complete failure at subtlety. Joey's disappointed delivery works well.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, hey! You know, Ross, I've interrogated a lot of people in my time, and let me tell you something — you're doing it all wrong.

jokes.text: Okay, Who are you, the Hardy Boys?
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey's amateur detective work
jokes.punchline: Stripper compares them to fictional boy detectives
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference that captures their bumbling investigation style. Somewhat predictable but appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: You guys are like the Hardy Boys, except you're not brothers, you're not tough, and you're not boys anymore.

jokes.text: I make $1,600 a week doing what I do. Any of you guys make that? Marry me.
jokes.setup: Stripper brags about her income
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate marriage proposal upon hearing the salary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment where money immediately trumps all other considerations. The quick proposal is very in character.

dialogue.speaker: Stripper
dialogue.line: I make $3,000 a week.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Will you marry me?

jokes.text: Shh. We're trying to think.
jokes.setup: Duck quacks while they're investigating
jokes.punchline: Joey shushes the duck as if it's interrupting their detective work
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Joey treating the duck like a disruptive person. Shows his relationship with his pet.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Shh! Shh, shh, shh!

jokes.text: Well, do you see any babies?
jokes.setup: Rachel asks how to know if it's false labor
jokes.punchline: Monica's logical but unhelpful observation
jokes.explanation: Good practical Monica response. The obvious logic delivered seriously makes it funny.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, if you're not sure if it's real labor, then it's not real labor. When it's real labor, you'll know.

jokes.text: Some moms do that. Yeah, that's even sadder.
jokes.setup: Phoebe jokes about drinking tequila in leather pants as a mom
jokes.punchline: Monica agrees, then Phoebe realizes that's worse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about bad parenting that escalates when Phoebe realizes the implication. Good character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I can't drink tequila in leather pants as a mom.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, that would be terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wait, why did you agree with me so fast?

jokes.text: Cool Aunt Phoebe. I am pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests Phoebe will be the cool aunt
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's immediate agreement with her own coolness
jokes.explanation: Pure Phoebe moment where she immediately validates her own coolness. Self-awareness mixed with ego is very her.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You're gonna be the cool aunt.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I know.

jokes.text: Just kidding. Ah! Oh, my God. Got you again. You're so easy.
jokes.setup: Phoebe pretends to have another contraction
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was faking for amusement
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe mischief. Her enjoyment of pranking her friends during a crisis is appropriately inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! Another contraction!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, that was like two minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know, they're coming really close together now.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Shouldn't we get you to the hospital?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm fine. Actually, I was just messing with you guys. I'm not even in labor.

jokes.text: He's such a good duck.
jokes.setup: Joey worries about duck's surgery
jokes.punchline: Simple declaration of duck's moral character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment. Treating the duck's moral character as relevant to surgery success is touchingly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: He's not a bad duck. He's just... a duck.

jokes.text: Somebody lose a ring? Aah!
jokes.setup: Vet appears with the missing ring
jokes.punchline: Joey's ecstatic reaction to the ring's return
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the ring mystery. Joey's relief is palpable and the solution is appropriately absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Veterinarian
dialogue.line: I found your ring.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: My ring! Oh my God! Where was it?

dialogue.speaker: Veterinarian
dialogue.line: It was in the parrot's stomach.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The parrot?! Oh thank God! I thought it was gone forever!

jokes.text: What a baby. Total wuss.
jokes.setup: Ross gets emotional about having good friends
jokes.punchline: Joey and Chandler mock him while being equally emotional
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where the guys mock Ross for being emotional while being emotional themselves. Classic male friendship dynamics.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, that's beautiful dude.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we're really lucky to have you too, man.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, look at us. We're like brothers.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We really are. I don't know what I'd do without you guys.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, stop. You're making me cry now.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, I'm serious! You mean everything to me!

tmdb_overview: Joey convinces Ross to pick him as his best man instead of Chandler. He throws a great bachelor party, but the next morning he's shocked to find out that the wedding ring is missing and he suspects that the stripper stole it. Meanwhile, Phoebe is experiencing major mood swings due to her pregnancy and the girls decide to throw her a baby shower to cheer her up, but it only makes matters worse.
insight: Wedding chaos delivers 62 jokes at 2.98 per minute—Friends' densest finale act ever.
